welcome.github_star: If you like Envis, give us a Star on GitHub
welcome.subtitle: A cross-platform environment management tool
welcome.rapid_setup_desc: When developing a new project, setting up the environment is often the hardest part. Download difficulties, tedious configuration, dependency errors - Envis solves these with one-click service installation, eliminating manual configuration and dependency troubleshooting, getting your project ready to run immediately.
welcome.multi_project_desc: When working on multiple projects, Project A needs Node14, Java8, MySQL, while another needs Node20, Java11, MariaDB. Switching between projects means switching environments every time, which is tedious. With Envis, switch environments with one click and easily manage complex project dependencies.
welcome.team_standardization_desc: Configuring environments on every computer is troublesome. Envis supports exporting and importing environment configurations, allowing one-click setup of identical environments. Eliminate the 'it works on my machine' problem, ensure all team members have consistent development environments, and guarantee stable project execution across different devices.
welcome.cmd_warning_title: Important Notice for Windows CMD Users
welcome.cmd_warning_desc:
Envis will set an AutoRun registry key pointing to a batch script, which loads environment variables when CMD starts.

All environment variables are session-only (set KEY=VALUE) and do NOT modify registry environment variables. We strongly recommend switching to PowerShell or WSL instead of CMD for a better experience.
welcome.feedback_desc: Submit bug reports or feature requests
welcome.join_community_desc: Scan QR code to join Envis user community
welcome.wechat_group_desc: Add on WeChat：zhang-4696，attach with "Envis" to join group

welcome.subtitle: A powerful, easy-to-use environment management tool for developers.
welcome.new_environment_desc: Create a new isolated environment for your project.
welcome.ai_assistant_desc: Get help with your configuration and troubleshooting.
welcome.documentation_desc: Read the comprehensive guides and API references.
welcome.rapid_setup_desc: Launch complex service stacks in seconds with pre-configured templates.
welcome.multi_project_desc: Switch between different project environments seamlessly without conflict.
welcome.team_standardization_desc: Share environment configurations to ensure consistency across your team.
welcome.step_1_desc: Click the plus button to create your first isolated environment.
welcome.step_2_desc: Configure services like databases, caches, and message queues.
welcome.step_3_desc: Start your services and monitor their status in real-time.
welcome.join_community_desc: Connect with other developers and get help.
welcome.wechat_group_desc: Scan the QR code to join

node_service.prefix_set_failed: Failed to set Node.js config prefix
node_service.pnpm_home_tooltip: Set the storage location for pnpm global packages
node_service.macos_silicon_warning_desc_1: Node.js v14 does not have an official macOS ARM64 (Apple Silicon) build, only x64. For macOS, when the system architecture is aarch64 (Apple Silicon) and the Node.js version is v14 or lower, Envis will automatically fallback to the x64 architecture package.
node_service.macos_silicon_warning_desc_2: Running x64 versions on Apple Silicon requires Rosetta 2. Please go to Finder -> Applications -> Utilities -> Terminal -> Right Click -> Get Info -> Open using Rosetta. Then reopen the terminal, and it will run in x64 architecture to ensure compatibility.

node_service.conflict_manager_desc: The following Node.js version managers were detected: {{managers}}. These tools may conflict with this application. It is recommended to uninstall or disable them before using this application.

python_service.uv_conflict_desc: uv is detected on your system. The current Python service is incompatible with uv, which may cause issues with environment variables, package installation, or command behavior. Please disable or uninstall uv.

nasm_service.title: NASM Assembler Tool
nasm_service.description: NASM is an assembler toolchain. Choose and install a NASM version in Add Service, then activate it to append binary paths into the current environment.

ssl_service.service_not_active_hint: Please activate the SSL service first
ssl_service.ca_not_initialized: CA not initialized, please initialize CA before issuing certificates
ssl_service.init_ca_desc: Configure CA information, this CA will be used to issue all local development certificates
ssl_service.ca_installed_desc: CA certificate is installed to the system, the browser can trust all certificates issued by this CA
ssl_service.ca_not_installed_desc: CA certificate is not installed to the system, the browser will display a certificate untrusted warning. Please follow the steps below to install the CA certificate:
ssl_service.macos_step1: Double-click the CA certificate file to open Keychain Access
ssl_service.macos_step3: Find the certificate in Keychain, double-click to open
ssl_service.macos_step4: Expand the "Trust" section, set "When using this certificate" to "Always Trust"
ssl_service.macos_step5: Close the window and enter the system password to confirm
ssl_service.windows_step1: Right-click the CA certificate file and select "Install Certificate"
ssl_service.windows_step3: Select "Local Machine" as the store location
ssl_service.windows_step4: Select "Place all certificates in the following store"
ssl_service.windows_step5: Click "Browse" and select "Trusted Root Certification Authorities"
ssl_service.tip1: After installing the CA certificate, all certificates issued by this CA will be automatically trusted
ssl_service.tip2: Only need to install once, newly issued certificates afterwards don't need to be installed again
ssl_service.tip3: It is recommended to regularly update the CA certificate to ensure security
ssl_service.no_certs_hint: Click the button above to issue a new certificate
ssl_service.delete_cert_desc: This will delete the certificate and all related files for {{domain}}, this action cannot be undone.
ssl_service.install_guide_1: First-time use requires adding the CA certificate to system trust
ssl_service.install_guide_2: Nginx: Use PEM format, configure ssl_certificate and ssl_certificate_key
ssl_service.install_guide_3: Apache: Use CRT + KEY, configure SSLCertificateFile and SSLCertificateKeyFile
ssl_service.install_guide_4: IIS: Import PFX file to certificate store
ssl_service.issue_cert_desc: Issue SSL/TLS certificate for local development domain
ssl_service.san_hint: Optional, used for certificates supporting multiple domains
